player_utterance: Quiet out here. You picked the good spot.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, the folded funeral program in his left hand, paper cup of cold coffee in his right

player_utterance: i am a distant friend, my name is Wasfi, I'm not here to make you talk. But I noticed you in there — your mouth didn't move on the amen.

believer_state_after.posture: standing still now, looking down at the program in his hand, no longer refolding it

player_utterance: i just came to check on you, and you look like you have something to say,,,,, Was it the words about him — or the words about you?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting down on the bench now, slowly, the program flat on his thigh

player_utterance: You don't have to answer. I'll just sit a minute, if that's alright.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, both hands resting on the program in his lap, looking out into the courtyard

voice_offers.read: He sat down; that's already trust. Don't crowd it.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to answer. I'll just sit a minute, if that's alright.

voice_offers.read: He teaches the words; ask which ones failed him today.
voice_offers.line: You teach this liturgy. Which line was it — the commendation, or the resurrection part?

voice_offers.read: He's softened an inch; name what he's hiding from.
voice_offers.line: You didn't say amen because you don't believe it for him, or because you don't believe it for you?

player_utterance: You knew what kind of man he was. And you sat anyway. That's the weight, isn't it?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, elbows on knees now, hands clasped, the program slipped to the seat beside him

voice_offers.read: He let me sit. The silence is doing the work.
Sitting forever lets him reseal. One small pin keeps the seam open.
voice_offers.line: You buried the amen before he was even in the ground. That's not nothing.

player_utterance: it rare to meet somebody who uses holy verses in there talk, its admirable, do you have more, that you can think of about this moment

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, picking the funeral program back up, smoothing it flat on his knee

voice_offers.read: He's bent forward now; the weight is named. Push the name further.
voice_offers.line: What kind of man, John. Say it out loud. You owe him that much, or you owe yourself.

voice_offers.read: He's leaned in, not away. Let him find the next word.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. I'm not going anywhere.

voice_offers.read: He sat through a liturgy he teaches. Ask what he was doing in it.
voice_offers.line: When you sat there — were you praying for him, or just running the words?

player_utterance: [SOURCE VOICE INVOKED: Matthew 25 — the least of these]

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, program flat on his knee, reading aloud, then lowering his head into one hand

voice_offers.read: Seeker just flattered him into a sermon; I want the knife back.
voice_offers.line: You're letting him hide in scripture again. Ask him what verse he couldn't say at the grave.

voice_offers.read: Flattery invites quotation; quotation can be pinned to a wound.
voice_offers.line: If one comes to mind for tonight specifically — not for the service, for him — I'd like to hear it.

player_utterance: I'm right here, John. The verse can take all the time it needs.

believer_state_after.posture: head in both hands now, elbows on knees, the program slipped down between his feet

voice_offers.read: He's broken open; pressing now would be cruelty.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to read anymore, John. Put it down.

voice_offers.read: The verse is the lever; name who he failed.
voice_offers.line: The least of these. Was he the least of these to you, John? Is that what you couldn't say?

player_utterance: [SOURCE VOICE INVOKED: Luke 16. The rich man and Lazarus]

believer_state_after.posture: sliding off the bench onto his knees in the dust of the courtyard, the program crushed under one hand, head bowed

voice_offers.read: He's collapsed inward; words now would be a hand on a wound.
He's finally undefended; one true question lands now or never.
voice_offers.line: What was his name to you, John? Not student. Not boy. What did you call him when no one was listening?
